title: Frank Miller
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Person |image=[[File:Frank_miller.jpg]] |name=Frank Miller |birth=[[January 27]], [[1950s|1957]]
[[Wikipedia:Olney, Maryland|Olney]], [[Wikipedia:Maryland|Maryland]], [[Wikipedia:United States|United States]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.rottentomatoes.com/celebrity/frank_miller|text=Frank Miller|work=[[Wikipedia:Rotten Tomatoes|Rotten Tomatoes]]|archivedate=20210119034355}} |death= |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] |occupation=*Artist *Director *Writer |companies= |website=[https://frankmillerink.com The Art of Frank Miller] }} '''Frank Miller''' (born [[January 27]], [[1950s|1957]]) is a comic book creator who drew a cover of an issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] comic series. Miller's first published work was in [[1978]] for [[Wikipedia:Gold Key Comics|Gold Key Comics]] on a comic based on the [[Wikipedia:The Twilight Zone|''The Twilight Zone'']] television series. He was soon working at [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC]] and [[Marvel Comics]], and in [[1979]] began illustrating [[Wikipedia:Daredevil (Marvel Comics)|''Daredevil'']], where he would further develop his style and become one of the more notable comic creators of the 1980s. In the late 1980s he wrote and illustrated [[Wikipedia:The Dark Knight Returns|''The Dark Knight Returns'']] and wrote [[Wikipedia:Batman: Year One|''Batman: Year One'']] for DC, stories which would help reshape the [[Wikipedia:Batman|Batman]] character for years to come. In the early 1990s Miller began creating comics for [[Dark Horse Comics]] including a series of stories titled [[Wikipedia:Sin City|''Sin City'']], a series that would eventually be made into a feature film. Miller wrote the screenplay and co-directed the [[Wikipedia:Sin City (film)|''Sin City'']] film, and has been involved in creating other films including writing the screenplays for [[Wikipedia:RoboCop 2|''RoboCop 2'']] and [[Wikipedia:RoboCop 3|''RoboCop 3'']] as well making cameo on-screen appearances in the films ''RoboCop 2'', [[Wikipedia:Daredevil (film)|''Daredevil'']], and ''Sin City''.
